SEARCH
Package server.db

Class DotEnv

java.lang.Object
server.db.DotEnv
public class DotEnv extends Object
Tiny .env loader for the server's database credentials, so secrets such as the MySQL password live in an untracked file rather than in source.

load() reads the first .env it can find and copies each KEY=value pair into the JVM's system properties (without overwriting any property already set on the command line), while also keeping them in an in-memory map readable via get(String) and loadInto(BiConsumer). The file is searched for in priority order: the path named by the gonature.env system property, a .env next to the running JAR or classes directory, a .env in the current working directory, and finally ~/.gonature.env.

Loading is best-effort: a missing or unreadable file is treated as absent so the server still starts. Lines are simple KEY=value pairs; blank lines and # comments are skipped, and surrounding single or double quotes around a value are stripped.

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    private static boolean
    loaded
    Whether load() has already run (it is idempotent).
    private static final Map<String,String>
    values
    Parsed key/value pairs from the .env file.

    Creates the .env loader (state and operations are static).

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    static String
    get(String key)
    Returns the value loaded for a key from the .env file.
    private static File
    jarSiblingEnv()
    Returns a .env beside the running JAR/classes dir, or null.
    static void
    load()
    Locates and parses the .env file (see the class description for the search order), copying every pair into the in-memory map and into system properties that are not already set.
    static void
    loadInto(BiConsumer<String,String> sink)
    Feeds every loaded key/value pair to the given consumer, letting callers forward the .env contents elsewhere (for example into another configuration object).

    locateEnvFile()
    Returns the first .env file found in the search order, or null.
    private static Map<String,String>
    parse(File file)
    Parses a .env file into key/value pairs (skips blanks and # comments; strips surrounding quotes).

  • Field Details

    • values

      private static final Map<String,String> values
      Parsed key/value pairs from the .env file.

    • loaded

      private static boolean loaded
      Whether load() has already run (it is idempotent).

  • Constructor Details

    • DotEnv

      public DotEnv()
      Creates the .env loader (state and operations are static).

  • Method Details

    • load

      public static void load()
      Locates and parses the .env file (see the class description for the search order), copying every pair into the in-memory map and into system properties that are not already set. Safe to call more than once: only the first call does any work.

    • get

      public static String get(String key)
      Returns the value loaded for a key from the .env file.
      Parameters:
      key - the key to look up
      Returns:
      the value parsed from the file, or null if the key was not present (or load() found no file)

    • loadInto

      public static void loadInto(BiConsumer<String,String> sink)
      Feeds every loaded key/value pair to the given consumer, letting callers forward the .env contents elsewhere (for example into another configuration object).
      Parameters:
      sink - receives each (key, value) pair currently loaded

    • locateEnvFile

      private static File locateEnvFile()
      Returns the first .env file found in the search order, or null.
      Returns:
      the first .env file found in the search order, or null

    • jarSiblingEnv

      private static File jarSiblingEnv()
      Returns a .env beside the running JAR/classes dir, or null.
      Returns:
      a .env beside the running JAR/classes dir, or null

    • parse

      private static Map<String,String> parse(File file)
      Parses a .env file into key/value pairs (skips blanks and # comments; strips surrounding quotes). Unreadable files yield an empty map.
      Parameters:
      file - the file to parse
      Returns:
      the parsed key/value pairs
